option.name: fileSummaryMaps
option.value: {"C:/Users/matil/PycharmProjects/Tetris":"{\"/GameFolders/Keyboard_Actions.py\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`movement`: Handles the movement of a figure based on user input (left, right, up, down).\\n\\n`check_collision_on_bottom`: Checks if the figure has collided with the bottom of the game board.\\n\\n`check_collision_on_sides`: Checks if the figure has collided with the sides of the game board.\\n\\n`simulate_movement`: Simulates the movement of a figure in a given direction (left, right, down, rotate).\\n\\n`undo_simulate_movement`: Undoes the simulated movement of a figure in a given direction.\\n\\nThis file is responsible for handling the movement and collision detection of a figure in a game.\",\"/GameFolders/Figure_Actions.py\":\"This file contains a set of classes and functions related to a Tetris-like game. Here\\u0027s a summary of each:\\n\\n`Figure`: Represents a Tetris figure, with properties such as its number, structure, color, and dimensions.\\n\\n`rotate_figure`: Rotates the given figure by modifying its structure and updating its dimensions.\\n\\n`undo_rotate_figure`: Restores the original structure of the given figure after a rotation.\\n\\n`choose_figure`: Randomly selects a Tetris figure from a predefined list.\\n\\nThe file also defines several color constants and a list of predefined Tetris figure structures.\",\"/GameFolders/Gameplay_Actions.py\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`clear_board()`: Clears the game board by setting all occupied cells to 0.\\n\\n`put_structure_on_board(figure, position)`: Puts a given figure on the game board at the specified position.\\n\\n`movement(figure, new_figure)`: Handles the movement of a figure on the game board, including checking if the figure has stopped.\\n\\n`check_if_stopped(figure)`: Checks if the given figure has stopped moving and should be placed on the game board.\\n\\n`update_col_heights()`: Updates the column heights on the game board.\\n\\nThis file appears to be part of a larger game implementation, providing functions to manage the game board and the movement of game pieces.\",\"/GameFolders/Main_Game.py\":\"This file appears to be a Pygame-based implementation of the classic game Tetris. It does not contain any classes, but it does have several functions that work together to create the game. Here\\u0027s a summary of the functions:\\n\\n`Drawing_Actions.draw_screen()`: Draws the game screen.\\n`Drawing_Actions.draw_figure(figure)`: Draws the current Tetris figure on the screen.\\n`Drawing_Actions.draw_next_figure(next_figure)`: Draws the next Tetris figure on the screen.\\n`Figure_Actions.choose_figure()`: Selects a random Tetris figure.\\n`Figure_Actions.Figure(Figure_Actions.choose_figure())`: Creates a new Tetris figure.\\n`Gameplay_Actions.movement(figure, True)`: Initializes the movement of the current Tetris figure.\\n`Gameplay_Actions.movement(figure, False)`: Moves the current Tetris figure down.\\n`Gameplay_Actions.update_col_heights()`: Updates the column heights in the game.\\n`Keyboard_Actions.movement(figure, pg, event)`: Handles keyboard input for moving the Tetris figure.\\n\\nThe file also includes a main game loop that handles events, updates the game state, and redraws the screen.\",\"/README.md\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided file. The file appears to be an empty XML document, which is a markup language used to structure and organize data. Without any content, the file does not perform any specific functionality.\",\"/GameFolders/Drawing_Actions.py\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`draw_screen()`: Draws the game screen, including the game board, next figure preview, and various lines and grids.\\n\\n`draw_figure(figure)`: Draws the current falling figure on the game board.\\n\\n`draw_next_figure(figure)`: Draws the next figure in the preview area.\\n\\nThis file is part of a Tetris game implementation, and it is responsible for the visual rendering of the game elements on the screen.\"}"}
